California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
William L. Corry 
P.O. Box 795 
Running Springs, CA 92382 
Dear Mr. Corry: 
January 26, 1988 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. I-88-035 
We have received your letter regarding the Arrowbear Park 
County Water District Board of Directors placing a tax override 
issue on the November ballot without the input from property 
owners. 
The Fair Political Practices commission has authority to 
interpret and enforce the Political Reform Act. 1/ The 
Political Reform Act was established for several purposes, some 
of which are disclosure of sources of campaign contributions, 
disclosure of assets and income of public officials, 
disqualification of public officials from decisions which could 
affect their private financial interests, and regulation of the 
activities of lobbyists to prevent improper influences on 
public officials. 
The question you pose in your letter is not covered by the 
Political Reform Act; therefore, we cannot advise you on this 
matter. 
In reviewing the material you submitted, it appears 
that you may be concerned about possible violations of the 
Brown Act. 
It would be more appropriate for you to direct your 
questions to your county district attorney. 
JET: jaj 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
/7

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804 .. 0807 • (916)322 .. 5660 


Jan. 9. 1988 
Arrowbear Lak~" I~ i)1 r~ od 
Ca. 
92382 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street Ste. 800 
Sacramento. 
Ca. 
95814 
Dear Sirs; 
I am writing this letter in the hope of getting information on how to 
correct a problem that I see in this area. It concerns taxation without 
representation. 
The area is a community. Arrowbear Lake, Ca. It consists of approximatly 
900 homes. The Arrowbear Park County Water District is a self governing 
Special District, with powers of water,sewer,and fire. It has a five man 
Board of Directors, elected by the registered voters of the District. 
There are about 240 registered voters, declared primary homes. This 
leaves about 750 homes with no vote on issues of their taxes or water & 
Sewer rates. 
I am enclosing copies of the minutes of the Districts (#1) Nay, 1987 and 
(#2) June. 1987 meetings. The minutes will show the Board of Directors 
did authorize a tax over-ride to be put on the November ballot and did 
not notify all taxpayer -
property ownners in time for their input or 
desires of need for same. I have hi~hlighted the items of my concern 
which mainly had to do with notification of all taxpayers. However, vli th 
the resonse that the Committee for Fair Taxation received I feel the 
possibility of a vote for all taxpayers in this type of District should 
be pursued. To 
sho~ you the type of response see the enclosed newspaper 
clippings. 
Any advice or help you can give me in regard to this matter will be 
appreciated. This Board has been notorius from it,s beginning for 
playing the surprize. They have telephone or one on one conversations 
reaching a concensus and then spring it at a Public meeting and not have 
much in the way of discussion. To point this out I am enclosing Copies 
of the March 14.1986 (#3) meeting Which I think is self explanetory 
I thank you for whatever help or advice you can give me. 
Yours truly, 


4 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
William L. Corry 
P.O. Box 795 
Running Springs, CA 
92382 
Dear Mr. Corry: 
January 15, 1988 
Re: 
88-035 
Your letter requesting advice under the political Reform 
Act was received on January 14, 1988 by the Fair political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact Jeanette Turvill, in the Legal 
Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, we will contact you 
shortly to advise you as to the information needed. 
If your 
request is for informal assistance, we will answer it as 
quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. 
Adm. Code Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
DMG:plh 
V_f!~Y truly yours, 
Ct'Lt¥' }~, 
(,,~> 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95~04~OB07 •

~AR PARK COUNTY WATER DISTRICT 
June 23, 1986 
FINANCE WORKSHOP 
At the hour of 6:05 p.m. the President of the Board of Directors called the 
workshop on finance to order. 
Directors present were, Arne Bjaanes, John 
Medearis, Max McIlravy and Baxter Williams. 
Others present were, Manager 
Wymer, Bill Carlisle, Chief Taylor, the secretary, and Jim Towns, Manager 
of Running Springs Water District. 
Mr. Carlisle informed the Board that the Directors and the attorney are 
responsible for the financial integrity of the District; and emphasized th 
irnportance of each board member understanding the finances of the District. 
Mr. Carlisle stated that you can not sit here and say O.K., you have to 
know what you are doing. 
In 1983 the Government Code 63646 was amended to 
charge the treasurer with the responsibility of providing on a monthly 
bas i s tot h e Boa r d, a b rea k do vi n 0 f all a c c 0 u n t.s • 
President Bjaanes asked if we can use the interest from funds in the 
assessment account for other purposes? 
Mr. Carlisle stated that he would 
give a opinion in writing as to this. 
Mr. Carlisle will also check vvith 
the district bond councel as to this matter. 
t 
Manager Wymer informed the Board that the Sewer 0 & M money is funds left 
from construction of the sewers, and can only be used for sewer related 
items. 
Director McIlravy suggested taking those funds and putting them 
aside for sewer treatment plant expansion. 
During discussion of the various funds and accounts, Director Williams 
sated that he felt a outside audit was in line. 
President Bjaanes 
that the books need to 
enable possible action 
There being no further business the meeting was adjourned at the hour of 
7:30 p.m. 
APPROVED:

QUESTION: s to your county district attorney. 
JET: jaj
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission William L. Corry P.O. Box 795 Running Springs, CA 92382 Dear Mr. Corry: January 26, 1988 Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance Our File No. I-88-035 We have received your letter regarding the Arrowbear Park County Water District Board of Directors placing a tax override issue on the November ballot without the input from property owners. The Fair Political Practices commission has authority to interpret and enforce the Political Reform Act. 1/ The Political Reform Act was established for several purposes, some of which are disclosure of sources of campaign contributions, disclosure of assets and income of public officials, disqualification of public officials from decisions which could affect their private financial interests, and regulation of the activities of lobbyists to prevent improper influences on public officials. The question you pose in your letter is not covered by the Political Reform Act; therefore, we cannot advise you on this matter. In reviewing the material you submitted, it appears that you may be concerned about possible violations of the Brown Act. It would be more appropriate for you to direct your questions to your county district attorney. JET: jaj Sincerely, Diane M. Griffiths General Counsel /7 ~/", 17 (-!fC{ iL(>CJ~(l:k~_Cl(Lo/ B~: Jeanette Turvill Legal Assistant 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804 .. 0807 • (916)322 .. 5660 Jan. 9. 1988 Arrowbear Lak~" I~ i)1 r~ od Ca. 92382 Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J Street Ste. 800 Sacramento. Ca. 95814 Dear Sirs; I am writing this letter in the hope of getting information on how to correct a problem that I see in this area. It concerns taxation without representation. The area is a community. Arrowbear Lake, Ca. It consists of approximatly 900 homes. The Arrowbear Park County Water District is a self governing Special District, with powers of water,sewer,and fire. It has a five man Board of Directors, elected by the registered voters of the District. There are about 240 registered voters, declared primary homes. This leaves about 750 homes with no vote on issues of their taxes or water & Sewer rates. I am enclosing copies of the minutes of the Districts (#1) Nay, 1987 and (#2) June. 1987 meetings. The minutes will show the Board of Directors did authorize a tax over-ride to be put on the November ballot and did not notify all taxpayer - property ownners in time for their input or desires of need for same. I have hi~hlighted the items of my concern which mainly had to do with notification of all taxpayers. However, vli th the resonse that the Committee for Fair Taxation received I feel the possibility of a vote for all taxpayers in this type of District should be pursued. To sho~ you the type of response see the enclosed newspaper clippings. Any advice or help you can give me in regard to this
